title: practical english usage - Part 243
content:
intonation: the voice goes down.

If the tag is not a real question - if we  are sure of the answer - we  use a falling The meetings at four o'clock, isn't it?

sure of the answer - we  use a rising intonation: the voice goes up.

If the tag is a real question - if we  really want to know something and are not In speech, we  can show the exact meaning of a question tag by the intonation.

5 meaning       and   intonation There's little we can do about it, is there?

It's hardly rained at all this summer,  has it?

It's no good, is it? (     ... isn^t-d?) not You never say what you're thinking do you? (     ... -don't you?) no, nobody, hardly, scarcely and little.

Non-negative tags are used after sentences containing negative words like never, 4 negative       words Speech and Spoken Exchanges   Section 27 grammar •  306   question tags: advanced points (It's a) nice day, isn’t it? (She was) talking to my husband, was she?

subjects and auxiliary verbs. (This is called ‘ ellipsis'. For details, ►    277.) In sentences with question tags, it is quite common to leave out pronoun 8  ellipsis:    Nice    day,    isn't    it?

I see. You don't like my cooking don't you?

Negative ‘ same-way' tags are occasionally heard; they usually sound aggressive.

I'll hold that for you, shall I?

I'll..., shall I? can be used to make offers.

You can eat shellfish, can you?

Your mother's at home, is she? This is the last bus, is it?

main sentence to make a guess, and then ask (in the tag) if it was correct.

‘ Same-way' tags can also be used to ask questions. In this structure, we  use the You think you're funny, do you?

So she thinks she's going to become a doctor, does she? Well, well.

So you're getting married, are you? How nice!

surprise, concern or some other reaction.

repeats what he/she has just learnt, and uses the tag to express interest, These are often used as responses to something that has been said: the speaker Non-negative question tags are quite common after affirmative sentences.

7 'same-way     ' question       tags:   You're      getting        married,         are    you?

For more about the use of do with have, ►    23-24.

Your father has a bad back, hasn't/doesn't he?

often both possible. (Do is normal in American English.) After non-auxiliary have (referring to states), question tags with have and do are 6 non-auxiliary         have Somebody wanted a drink, didn 't they? Who was it?

Nobody phoned, did they?

(and no one, etc).

We   use they (►   175) to refer to nobody, somebody and everybody Nothing can happen, can it?

We   use it in question tags to refer to nothing and everything.

5  it  and   they     with    nothing,         nobody,        somebody,           etc There's some more chairs upstairs, aren't there?

When  there's introduces a plural noun phrase (►    130.6), the tag is aren't there?

There weren't any problems, were there?

There's something wrong isn't there?

There can be used in question tags.

4 There's      ..., isn't    there?

Let's have a party, shall we?

After let's ... (in suggestions, etc, ►    225), we  use shall we?

3 Let's   ..., shall      we?

grammar •  307   reply questions: Was it? Did you, dear?

’ She's put on a lot of weight.* ’ Yes, hasn't she?

’ It was a lovely concert.* ’ Yes, wasn't it? I did enjoy it.' agreement (like negative-question exclamations, ►    223.4).

Negative questions in reply to affirmative statements express emphatic ’ I don't understand.’ ’ Don'tyou? I'm sorry.' ’Jack likes that girl next door.’ ’ Oh,  does he?

’ I've got a headache.’ ’ Have you, dear? I'll get you an aspirin.’ ’ We had a lovely trip.* ’ Didyou? ’ Yes, we went...’ listener is reacting to what has been said. More examples: Note that these questions do not ask for information - they simply show that the ’ It was a terrible party.’ ’ Was it? ’ Yes ...’ like question tags (►  305).

attention and interested. They  are constructed with auxiliary verb + pronoun, Short questions are often used in conversation to show that the listener is paying 307 reply    questions:      Was   it?  Did   you,    dear?
